channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former Syrian president Assad is video gaming in a luxury Moscow apartment: Report
item.description: Syria’s former President Bashar al-Assad, whose notoriously brutal rule over the country earned him the nickname “The Butcher,” was deposed in 2024 after years of bloody civil war.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: TSA CIO, State senior procurement executive moving on
item.description: Yemi Oshinnaiye, the Transportation Security Administration’s CIO, is returning to industry after more than three years leading the agency’s technology office. The post TSA CIO, State senior procurement executive moving on first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How to watch Belgium vs North Macedonia for *FREE*
item.description: Here's our quick guide on how to watch Belgium vs North Macedonia for *FREE* in the UEFA World Cup 2026 Qualifiers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Tesla’s China shipments rise at start of busy sales season
item.description: Shipments from Tesla Inc.'s Shanghai factory increased in September as China's car market kicks off its busy sales period and automakers start their final push to meet annual targets.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers leak children’s data in major nursery breach
item.description: Hacker group Radiant stole data from 8,000 children at Kido nursery chain, demanding ransom and directly contacting parents with intimidation tactics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Deal with the Devil? Free VPNs still widespread across the UK
item.description: A survey conducted by NordVPN reveals that 12% of British VPN users rely on free VPN services, underestimating associated risks.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Iacovcich, TechRadar

item.title: Google researchers say Oracle EBR hackers have hit dozens of organizations
item.description: The attacks started a lot earlier than previously thought, and bear the hallmarks of multiple threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Beyond Ping and SNMP: Building an AI-ready observability framework for enterprise networks
item.description: The problem How many times in a year do we get complaints from our end users that the network is slow…and our monitoring tool has not yet alerted us to anything critical? Sometimes Zoom lags, and other times Jira takes a lifetime to load — and you don’t know whether it’s a circuit issue or a Wi-Fi i
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Real technology transformation starts with empowering people and teams
item.description: Ninety percent of the conversations I’ve had with senior leaders and technology stakeholders over the past 12 months have started with AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Saying 'I do' to AI? Ohio lawmaker proposes ban on marriage, legal personhood for AI
item.description: “As the computer systems improve in their capacity to act more like humans, we want to be sure we have prohibitions in our law that prohibit those systems from ever being human in their agency.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: In the age of AI, websites will be transformed
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Too many junk calls to your iPhone? Try Apple’s new iOS 26 call screen feature
item.description: iPhone users have a new tool to combat the scourge of nuisance phone calls: a virtual gatekeeper that can screen incoming calls from unknown numbers. It’s among the bevy of new features that Apple rolled out with last month’s release of iOS 26.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google’s Jules coding agent adds CLI, API
item.description: Google Labs has introduced Jules Tools and the Jules API, a command-line interface and API respectively for the Jules coding agent. Jules Tools and the Jules API were announced October 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon sets release date for Melania Trump documentary
item.description: A high-profile documentary on first lady Melania Trump now has a release date, with Amazon MGM Studios saying it'll premiere in theaters in January. "Melania" will be released on Jan. 30 in U.S. theaters before streaming on Prime Video, the media giant announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Dominion Voting Systems sold and renamed as Liberty Vote
item.description: Dominion Voting Systems, the voting technology company that was at the center of controversy and false stolen-election claims after President Trump’s 2020 loss, has been sold and will be renamed as Liberty Vote, its new ownership announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Tesla hit with probe after crashes involving a self-driving feature that Musk has boasted about
item.description: Federal regulators have opened yet another investigation into Tesla’s so-called full-self driving technology after dozens of incidents in which the electric vehicle maker’s cars ran red lights or drove on the wrong side of the road, sometimes crashing into other vehicles and endangering drivers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leveraging AI-powered defense to secure the NOC at Black Hat USA
item.description: The Black Hat USA Network Operations Center (NOC) is a unique environment, serving as a real-time proving ground for cybersecurity technologies tested by some of the world’s most talented cyber professionals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Accelerating AI success with Cloudera AMPs
item.description: AI is moving fast, but turning pilots into production takes more than excitement. It demands skilled teams, the right infrastructure, and significant time. Large enterprises can often throw resources at the challenge, but most businesses are looking for a smarter, faster path to measurable AI value.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Princess of Wales suggests parents should put down the phone to combat social discontent
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales has a suggestion for parents: Please put down the phone. Kate, as she is commonly known, collaborated with adult development researcher Robert Waldinger to warn that technology is contributing to an epidemic of disconnection that is hurting family relationships.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Starburst pushes lakehouse boundaries with multi-agent AI and unified vector search
item.description: Starburst is updating its AI services to help enterprises build multi-agent workflows and monitor these agents for compliance and cost effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Greenland's leader hails EU as trusted friend and urges investment in its minerals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Greenland’s leader on Wednesday thanked the European Union for being “a trusted friend” and urged the 27-nation bloc to invest in his country’s mineral resources, renewable energy, airports and internet services.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USPTO to use AI tool for automated search pilot
item.description: The USPTO continues to invest heavily in AI capabilities; a new pilot program is designed to assess patent applications for potential "prior art" issues. The post USPTO to use AI tool for automated search pilot first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: React JS library moving from Meta to the Linux Foundation
item.description: React, a popular open source JavaScript library for web and native user interfaces, will be transferred from Meta to the React Foundation, a new organization being formed under the Linux Foundation. The new foundation will be the home for React, React Native, and supporting projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nobel Prize in Physics awarded to quantum pioneers
item.description: In a boost to the profile of quantum computing, the Nobel Prize Committee has awarded this year’s Physics prize to three pioneers in the field of quantum research: John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret and John M.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's Play Store shake-up looms after Supreme Court refuses to delay overhaul of the monopoly
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday refused to protect Google from a year-old order requiring a major makeover of its Android app store that's designed to unleash more competition against a system that a jury declared an illegal monopoly.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI and chipmaker AMD sign chip supply partnership for AI infrastructure
item.description: Semiconductor maker AMD will supply its chips to artificial intelligence company OpenAI as part of an agreement to team up on building AI infrastructure, the companies said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia and Fujitsu team for vertical industry AI projects
item.description: Nvidia has partnered with Japanese technology giant Fujitsu to work together on vertical industry-specific artificial intelligence projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 7 SASE certifications to validate converged network and security skills
item.description: As cyberattacks grow more sophisticated and AI-powered threats escalate, enterprises are under pressure to evolve beyond traditional perimeter-based network security.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Third of Americans romantically linked with AI, survey finds
item.description: Out of more than 1,000 U.S. adults, 28% were intimate or romantically linked to the system, according to the study.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation
